skill.name: designing-components
skill.description: Design frontend components with separate CSS files, Tailwind directives, and project-specific component placement rules.
skill.summary: Guides creation of Tailwind-styled components using scoped CSS files and component-layer conventions.
skill.author: Emz1998

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command and weak cryptography patterns, but review found only Markdown inline code, Tailwind directive names, and documentation file paths. No executable code, network access, secret handling, prompt injection, or cryptographic implementation was found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Inline Code Flagged as Commands
low_findings.description: The flagged locations contain Tailwind directive names and project file paths inside Markdown guidance. They do not execute shell commands or Ruby backticks.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is limited to Markdown inline code and bullet text in SKILL.md. There is no script block, command runner, or instruction to execute the path strings.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The flagged lines contain metadata, prose, and a Markdown file path. No hashing, encryption, signing, or cryptographic API usage is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed text contains no cryptographic operation or security-sensitive transformation. The analyzer appears to match substrings in normal Markdown content.

content.value_statement: Component styling can drift when structure and CSS rules are inconsistent. This skill guides agents to create components with separate CSS files and Tailwind component-layer conventions.
actual_capabilities: Guides creation of a separate CSS file for each component.
Instructs agents to style components with Tailwind @apply usage.
Uses @layer components for parent and child component styles.
Directs agents to read local Tailwind 4.1 context documents when present.
Supports custom utility and variant guidance for component-specific styles.
Restricts new component work to the configured component root.
limitations: The skill provides guidance only and does not include executable tooling.
The component directory path is project-specific and may need confirmation.
It depends on local Tailwind context files that may not exist in every repository.
It does not define accessibility, testing, or interaction behavior requirements.

use_cases.title: Create a New Product Component
use_cases.description: Generate a component and matching CSS file that follow local Tailwind component-layer rules.

use_cases.title: Standardize Component Styling
use_cases.description: Move scattered utility choices into a consistent component CSS pattern for easier maintenance.

use_cases.title: Apply Tailwind 4.1 Project Guidance
use_cases.description: Use local Tailwind reference documents before changing component styling decisions.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Component
prompt_templates.scenario: Create a simple component with matching styles.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new profile summary component using this skill. Include a separate CSS file and follow the project component directory rules.

prompt_templates.title: Styled Variant
prompt_templates.scenario: Add styling states for a component.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a notification banner component with success, warning, and error variants. Use Tailwind component-layer guidance from this skill.

prompt_templates.title: Project Context Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Use local Tailwind 4.1 documents before implementation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Before creating a pricing card component, read the local Tailwind 4.1 context files referenced by this skill and apply their guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor Existing Component
prompt_templates.prompt: Refactor this existing component to use a separate CSS file, nested component-layer classes, and project-safe component placement rules.

output_examples.input: Create a user card component for an account settings page.
output_examples.output: A component plan with the target component path, matching CSS file, Tailwind layer strategy, and parent-child styling notes.

output_examples.input: Refactor a button group that uses repeated utility classes.
output_examples.output: A concise refactor summary that moves repeated styling into component-layer classes and preserves the existing UI behavior.

output_examples.input: Design a component after reading Tailwind 4.1 local context.
output_examples.output: A design response that references relevant local guidance, explains style choices, and avoids protected UI component files.

best_practices: Confirm the component root path before creating files.
Read local Tailwind context documents when the repository provides them.
Keep component CSS focused on the component and its direct child elements.
anti_patterns: Do not edit protected shared UI component files without explicit approval.
Do not scatter component styling across unrelated global files.
Do not skip local Tailwind guidance when it exists in the repository.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create frontend components with separate CSS files and Tailwind component-layer styling conventions.

faq.question: Does this skill run commands?
faq.answer: No. It contains Markdown instructions only and does not include executable scripts.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use it?
faq.answer: It is marked for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code compatibility.

faq.question: Does it require Tailwind CSS?
faq.answer: Yes. Its guidance is specific to Tailwind directives and Tailwind 4.1 context documents.

faq.question: Can it modify shared UI primitives?
faq.answer: The skill instructs agents not to touch the protected shared UI component directory.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. Security review found no confirmed malicious behavior or executable dangerous pattern.
